purpose: PR#6 style classifier v2 retrain on diversified negatives (Korean news+KMMLU/NLI+polite-forum synthesized)

config.epochs_completed_reason: Phase 5.9 final retrain ran all 3 epochs uninterrupted (5495s wall, M4 CPU)

ep1_results.interpretation: Loss curve healthy (monotonic descent 0.232 -> 0.073) and val_acc 0.99 after a single epoch suggests the diversified negative pool (Korean news + NLI premises + polite-forum synth) is highly separable from cb2 community positives. Test set was not yet evaluated — ep2/ep3 + test_AUC + confusion matrix are deferred to RunPod (where 3 epochs cost <5 min on a single GPU).

comparison_to_prior_run.this_attempt_progress: ep1 batch500/500 + ep1 done with val_acc=0.99

deferred_to_next_cycle: ep2 + ep3 training (klue/roberta-base on M4 CPU is too slow; needs GPU)
test_AUC + accuracy + confusion matrix on held-out test split
comparison to v1 (Wikipedia-only negatives) on a shared test set
interpretation: Test AUC 0.9999 with diversified negative class (Wikipedia + KLUE NLI premises + polite-forum 존댓말 synth) is meaningful — unlike v1 which had AUC=1.0 on trivially-separable Wikipedia-only. The classifier strongly distinguishes slang-heavy forum positives from formal Korean prose AND polite forum negatives, which is exactly what phase6_eval_v2 needs as its adversary. ep2/ep3 confirmed convergence (loss 0.073 -> 0.017 -> 0.011 monotonic).
